# MCP Tool Reference

> Auto-generated by AI OS. Do not edit manually — this file is regenerated on each refresh.
> Generated: 2026-04-29T07:35:19.860Z

Active tools for this project: **27** of 30 total.

| Tool | Description | Parameters (`*` = required) |
|---|---|---|
| `search_codebase` | Search for patterns, symbols, or text across the project codebase. Respects .gitignore. Returns matching file paths and snippets. | `query`*: The pattern or text to search for<br>`filePattern`: Optional glob pattern to limit search (e.g. "*.ts", "src/**/*.py")<br>`caseSensitive`: Whether search is case-sensitive (default: false) |
| `get_project_structure` | Returns an annotated file tree of the project (respects .gitignore, skips node_modules/build/dist). Useful for understanding project layout before making changes. | `depth`: Max directory depth to show (default: 4)<br>`path`: Subdirectory to start from (default: project root) |
| `get_conventions` | Returns the detected coding conventions for this project: naming rules, file structure, testing patterns, forbidden practices. | — |
| `get_stack_info` | Returns the complete tech stack inventory: languages, frameworks, key dependencies, build tools, and test setup. | — |
| `get_file_summary` | Returns a structured summary of a specific file: key exports, types, functions, and brief description. Token-efficient alternative to reading the full file. | `filePath`*: Path to the file relative to project root |
| `get_env_vars` | Returns all required environment variable names (from .env.example or code). Shows which are set vs. missing. Never returns values. | — |
| `get_package_info` | Returns installed package versions and direct dependencies. Useful before suggesting library usage to avoid API mismatch. | `packageName`: Optional: specific package to look up (e.g. "@trpc/server") |
| `get_impact_of_change` | Shows what files are affected when a given file changes. Returns direct importers and all transitively affected files. | `filePath`*: File path relative to project root (e.g. "src/types.ts") |
| `get_dependency_chain` | Shows the full dependency chain for a file: what it imports and what imports it, with export names. | `filePath`*: File path relative to project root (e.g. "src/utils/auth.ts") |
| `check_for_updates` | Checks if the AI OS artifacts installed in this repo are out of date. Returns update instructions when a newer version of AI OS is available. | — |
| `get_memory_guidelines` | Returns repository memory rules and memory usage protocol from .github/ai-os/context/memory.md. | — |
| `get_repo_memory` | Retrieves persisted repository memory entries from .github/ai-os/memory/memory.jsonl, optionally filtered by query/category. | `query`: Optional full-text query against title/content/tags<br>`category`: Optional category filter (e.g. architecture, conventions, pitfalls)<br>`limit`: Max entries to return (default: 10, max: 50) |
| `remember_repo_fact` | Stores a durable repository memory entry in .github/ai-os/memory/memory.jsonl using dedupe/upsert rules (marks superseded conflicts and avoids duplicate facts). | `title`*: Short memory title<br>`content`*: Durable fact/decision/constraint<br>`category`: Category (e.g. conventions, architecture, build, testing, security)<br>`tags`: Optional comma-separated tags |
| `get_active_plan` | Returns the persisted active session plan from .github/ai-os/memory/session/active-plan.json. Use after context resets to restore goals and avoid drift. | — |
| `upsert_active_plan` | Creates or updates the persisted active plan (objective, criteria, current/next step, blockers). This provides durable task state across context resets. | `objective`*: Primary goal for the current task<br>`acceptanceCriteria`*: Success criteria for task completion<br>`status`: Plan status: active, paused, or completed<br>`currentStep`: Current execution step<br>`nextStep`: Next planned action<br>`blockers`: Optional blockers, comma-separated or newline-separated |
| `append_checkpoint` | Appends a progress checkpoint to .github/ai-os/memory/session/checkpoints.jsonl to preserve intent and execution state during long tool-call sequences. | `title`*: Checkpoint title<br>`status`: Checkpoint status: open or closed (default: open)<br>`notes`: Optional checkpoint notes<br>`toolCallCount`: Optional tool call count snapshot at checkpoint time |
| `close_checkpoint` | Closes an existing checkpoint by id in .github/ai-os/memory/session/checkpoints.jsonl. | `checkpointId`*: Checkpoint id returned by append_checkpoint<br>`notes`: Optional closing notes to append |
| `record_failure_pattern` | Records or updates a failure pattern in .github/ai-os/memory/session/failure-ledger.jsonl to prevent repeating the same mistakes. | `tool`*: Tool or subsystem where failure occurred<br>`errorSignature`*: Short normalized error signature<br>`rootCause`*: Suspected or confirmed root cause<br>`attemptedFix`*: Fix that was attempted<br>`outcome`: Result of the fix: unresolved, partial, or resolved<br>`confidence`: Confidence in diagnosis from 0.0 to 1.0 |
| `compact_session_context` | Creates a compact session summary from active plan, open checkpoints, and recent failure patterns to reduce context stuffing and preserve continuity. | — |
| `get_session_context` | Returns the compact session context card with MUST-ALWAYS rules, build/test commands, and key file locations. CALL THIS at the start of every new conversation to reload critical context after a session reset. | — |
| `get_recommendations` | Returns stack-appropriate recommendations: MCP servers, VS Code extensions, agent skills, and GitHub Copilot Extensions. Useful for setting up a new developer environment. | — |
| `suggest_improvements` | Analyzes project structure and memory entries to return architectural and tooling optimization suggestions (e.g. missing env var documentation, undocumented key paths, skills gaps). | — |
| `set_watchdog_threshold` | Configures the automatic watchdog checkpoint interval for the current session (default: 8 tool calls). Increase for complex multi-step tasks; decrease for shorter focused work. Range: 1–100. | `threshold`*: Number of tool calls between automatic watchdog checkpoints (1–100) |
| `reset_session_state` | Clears all session state files (active-plan.json, checkpoints.jsonl, failure-ledger.jsonl, runtime-state.json, compact-context.md) so a new branch or task starts from a clean slate. Durable repo memory (memory.jsonl) is never modified. | — |
| `sync_hosted_memory` | Returns guidance and a prompt template for mirroring durable facts from Copilot hosted/in-context memory into .github/ai-os/memory/memory.jsonl. Lists existing entries to prevent duplication. | — |
| `get_context_freshness` | Computes a freshness score (0–100) for AI OS context artifacts by comparing them against the stored context snapshot. Returns a list of stale artifacts, changed source files, and targeted sync recommendations. Run after structural code changes to detect context drift. | — |
| `prune_memory` | Compacts the repository memory file by running full hygiene (near-duplicate detection, TTL enforcement, superseded entry removal) and physically deleting all stale entries. Returns a maintenance summary with counts of removed vs. kept entries. | — |

## Usage

Call these tools from any Copilot agent or chat session. Tools with a `condition` are only
active when the matching stack dependency is detected in your project.

> Tip: Call `get_session_context` first to reload MUST-ALWAYS rules before invoking other tools.
